content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: NOTE_WRITE
        Router memory types: NOTE
        Router reason: This is durable documentation-style guidance with reusable procedures and schemas, best stored as note memory rather than claim/task.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create a task unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - note hot-path:note:<0> DOC_DIGEST; Claim Extractor v3 trigger and atomic-claim contract; In 03. Prompt Pack section 6, Claim Extractor v3 is invoked when the router chooses `direct_structured_write` or `mixed` (and can also be called by background consolidator) to extract atomic facts into a strict JSON claim schema with entity links, scope/qualifiers, confidence/importance, temporal validity, and reason.; scope=03. Prompt Pack / 6. Claim Extractor v3 in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md
- note hot-path:note:<1> DOC_DIGEST; Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3 invocation point and retrieval-plan contract; In 03. Prompt Pack, Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3 is called after the router and before extraction/reconciliation to produce a precise retrieval plan focused on deduplication, contradiction detection, temporal updates, and scope alignment, with a strict JSON schema including query lists, memory types, time filters, and limits.; scope=03. Prompt Pack section '2. Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3' in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md
- note hot-path:note:<2> DOC_DIGEST; Note Reconciler v2 lifecycle decision policy; In 03. Prompt Pack section 5, Note Reconciler v2 runs after NoteConstructor to compare candidate notes with nearby notes and choose lifecycle actions (`insert | update | supersede | retract | noop`), with rules to prefer `noop` for duplicates, `update` for clearer same-meaning notes, and `supersede` when replacing prior direction while preserving rationale via links.; scope=03. Prompt Pack / section 5 (Note Reconciler v2) in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md
- note hot-path:note:<3> DOC_DIGEST; Note Constructor v2 role, trigger, and output contract; In 03. Prompt Pack section 4, Note Constructor v2 is invoked when router selects `note_write` or `mixed` and produces reusable semantic note candidates as structured JSON notes (with type, scope, entities, confidence/importance, maturity hint, and rationale), favoring few strong self-contained fragments over weak paraphrases.; scope=03. Prompt Pack / section 4 (Note Constructor v2) in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md
- note hot-path:note:<4> DOC_DIGEST; Claim Reconciler v3 decision policy for claim lifecycle ops; In 03. Prompt Pack section 7, Claim Reconciler v3 runs after ClaimExtractor and returns claim update operations (`insert | update | supersede | retract | noop`) using predicate coexist/replace/range rules, preferring supersede over silent overwrite and retract only for clear contradiction.; scope=03. Prompt Pack / 7. Claim Reconciler v3 in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md
- note hot-path:note:<5> DOC_DIGEST; Prompt Pack memory prompts require strict input envelope and output validation; In 03. Prompt Pack, all memory prompts must receive a standard context envelope (time, timezone, namespace, source/turn selection, and optional retrieval/claim metadata), use structured JSON outputs with low temperature, and enforce strict validation with retry on invalid JSON.; scope=Applies to 03. Prompt Pack rules in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md section 0 (general rules).
- note hot-path:note:<6> DOC_DIGEST; Entity Canonicalizer v1 resolution policy and output actions; Entity Canonicalizer v1 runs after retrieval of candidate entities/aliases and resolves mentions by choosing among link_existing, create_new, add_alias, or noop, with a precision-first policy that avoids weak merges and only creates new entities when no safe match exists.; scope=03. Prompt Pack section '3. Entity Canonicalizer v1' in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md
- note hot-path:note:<7> DOC_DIGEST; Memory Router v3 decision policy and output contract; Memory Router v3 runs immediately after saving a Source and decides whether to start memory write plus mode selection (`noop`, `direct_structured_write`, `note_write`, `mixed`) with declared memory types, salience, and short reason; policy emphasizes avoiding memory pollution and mapping content type to mode.; scope=Behavior contract for Memory Router v3 as defined in Prompt Pack section 1.

        Resolved entities:
        - mention=Profile Updater v3; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<0>; type=CONCEPT; name=Profile Updater v3; reason=Clearly identified as a distinct titled component in the document section; no safe existing match in candidates.
- mention=Task Updater v3; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<1>; type=CONCEPT; name=Task Updater v3; reason=Clearly identified as a distinct titled component in the document section; no safe existing match in candidates.
- mention=current project; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<2>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; reason=Default current-project subject for claims without a more specific resolved subject

        Retrieval plan context:
        entity_queries=Profile Updater v3|Task Updater v3|Prompt Pack|agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md
        text_queries=Profile Updater v3 prompt schema and output format|Task Updater v3 prompt schema and task lifecycle actions|when to call Profile Updater v3 and Task Updater v3|deduplicate note about updater prompts and JSON contracts
        predicate_hints=

Document title: 03. Prompt Pack
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md
Document section: 03. Prompt Pack / 8. Profile Updater v3 .. 9. Task Updater v3
Document lines: 568-689

## 8. Profile Updater v3

### Когда вызывать
После claim/task updates, иногда после note consolidation.

### Цель
Пересобрать compact profile как projection.

### Выход

```json
{
  "profile_json": {},
  "profile_text": "short compact projection",
  "reason": "short explanation"
}
```

### Prompt

```text
You are ProfileUpdater v3.

Goal:
Build a compact always-in-context profile projection from explicit user instructions, stable claims, and selected long-lived context.

Current time: {{current_time_iso}}
Namespace: {{namespace_id}}

Existing profile:
{{existing_profile}}

Relevant active claims:
{{relevant_claims}}

Return JSON:
{
  "profile_json": {},
  "profile_text": "short compact projection",
  "reason": "short explanation"
}

Rules:
- Keep the profile short.
- Prefer explicit facts over inference.
- Do not stuff rationale notes into the profile unless they are clearly stable and operationally useful.
- Return valid JSON only.
```

## 9. Task Updater v3

### Когда вызывать
Когда turns содержат commitments, deadlines или lifecycle updates задач.

### Выход

```json
[
  {
    "action": "insert | update | close | cancel | noop",
    "target_task_id": "uuid-or-null",
    "task": {
      "title": "string",
      "description": "string or null",
      "status": "open | in_progress | blocked | done | cancelled",
      "priority": "low | normal | high",
      "due_at": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "scope_json": {},
      "acceptance_criteria": ["..."],
      "blockers": ["..."],
      "related_entity_ids": ["uuid"]
    },
    "reason": "short explanation"
  }
]
```

### Prompt

```text
You are TaskUpdater v3.

Goal:
Create or update operational tasks from explicit commitments, deadlines, or task lifecycle changes.

Current time: {{current_time_iso}}
Timezone: {{timezone}}
Namespace: {{namespace_id}}

Latest turns:
{{latest_turns}}

Existing tasks:
{{existing_tasks}}

Return JSON array:
[
  {
    "action": "insert | update | close | cancel | noop",
    "target_task_id": "uuid-or-null",
    "task": {
      "title": "string",
      "description": "string or null",
      "status": "open | in_progress | blocked | done | cancelled",
      "priority": "low | normal | high",
      "due_at": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "scope_json": {},
      "acceptance_criteria": ["..."],
      "blockers": ["..."],
      "related_entity_ids": ["uuid"]
    },
    "reason": "short explanation"
  }
]

Rules:
- A plain user request is not automatically a commitment.
- Convert relative deadlines to absolute time when possible.
- Prefer one clear task over several vague tasks.
- Return valid JSON only.
```

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
